docs.title: maldet
docs.text:
Plug-and-play framework for building malware detectors in Python.

Six composable layers — Reader, Extractor, Model, Trainer, Evaluator, Predictor — connected by runtime-checkable Protocols. Classical ML (scikit-learn) and deep learning (PyTorch Lightning) are first-class, side-by-side. A single framework-owned CLI — maldet run, describe, check, scaffold — is the only thing platforms need to invoke.

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install maldet[lightning,mlflow]

docs.title: Get started
docs.text: See Quickstart.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: maldet is a composition framework. Each detector is a pipeline assembled from six layers; each layer has a single responsibility and a well-defined interface (a typing.Protocol).

docs.title: The six layers
docs.text:
1. SampleReader — produces an iterable of Sample(sha256, path, label, metadata). The builtin SampleCsvReader reads the sample_csv contract: file_name[,label] columns, sample at samples_root/<sha[:2]>/<sha>.
  2. FeatureExtractor — transforms one Sample into an ndarray or tensor. Stateless, pure, cacheable.
  3. Model — the estimator or nn.Module. Contains no I/O, no training logic.
  4. Trainer — runs fit() and owns serialization. Two engines: SklearnTrainer (joblib) and LightningTrainer (ckpt + DDP-ready).
  5. Evaluator — computes metrics into a MetricReport. Builtin BinaryClassification uses sklearn.metrics.
  6. Predictor — batch predictions into the standardized predictions.csv shape. Online mode is deferred.

docs.title: Control plane
docs.text:
The maldet CLI is the only entry point. It reads maldet.toml (manifest) and a Hydra YAML config, instantiates the declared layers, and drives a single stage.

See Protocols and Stages & Manifest for the interface details.

docs.title: Config (Hydra)
docs.text: maldet uses Hydra for configuration. Each stage reads a single YAML file passed via --config.

docs.title: Required top-level keys
docs.text:
defaults: [_self_]
stage: train          # train | evaluate | predict

paths:
  config_dir: .
  output_dir: ./output
  samples_root: ./samples
  source_model: ./output/model

data:
  train_csv: ./train.csv
  test_csv: ./test.csv
  predict_csv: ./predict.csv

model:
  _target_: mydet.models.make_rf
  n_estimators: 100

model._target_ is resolved by hydra.utils.instantiate. Any extra keys under model: become keyword arguments to the factory function.

docs.title: Path injection
docs.text:
paths.output_dir, paths.samples_root, and paths.source_model are injected by the StageRunner before each stage. Override on the command line:

maldet run train --config config.yaml \
  paths.output_dir=/mnt/runs/001 \
  paths.samples_root=/data/elf

docs.title: CLI overrides
docs.text:
Any config key can be overridden with dot-notation:

maldet run train --config config.yaml model.n_estimators=200

docs.title: Multirun sweeps
docs.text:
Hydra multirun is triggered with --multirun (or -m):

maldet run train --config config.yaml --multirun \
  model.n_estimators=50,100,200

Each combination runs in its own subdirectory under output_dir. Combine with hydra/launcher=joblib for local parallelism.

docs.title: Event Stream
docs.text: Every stage emits structured events through the EventLogger protocol. The CompositeEventLogger fans out to three sinks simultaneously: JSONL file, stdout, and MLflow.

docs.title: Event kinds
docs.text:
Kind Required fields stage_begin stage stage_end stage, status data_loaded (none) epoch_begin epoch epoch_end epoch metric name, value artifact_written path checkpoint_saved path warning message error message

Payload validation is enforced by maldet.events.kinds.validate_payload. Extra fields are allowed and round-trip transparently.

docs.title: JSONL format
docs.text:
Each line in events.jsonl is a JSON object with a leading ts (ISO-8601 UTC) timestamp followed by kind and the event payload:

{"ts": "2026-04-24T08:00:01.234567Z", "kind": "stage_begin", "stage": "train"}
{"ts": "2026-04-24T08:00:01.300000Z", "kind": "data_loaded", "n_train": 50000}
{"ts": "2026-04-24T08:00:05.100000Z", "kind": "metric", "name": "val_accuracy", "value": 0.9812}
{"ts": "2026-04-24T08:00:05.200000Z", "kind": "stage_end", "stage": "train", "status": "success"}

Each line is fsynced immediately, so a pod kill does not lose buffered events.

docs.title: Stdout sink
docs.text: The StdoutEventLogger prefixes each line with maldet.event: so platform log collectors can filter maldet events from application logs.

docs.title: Protocols
docs.text:
All six layers plus the event logger are defined as @runtime_checkable typing.Protocol classes in maldet.protocols. Implementations do not need to inherit — structural typing is used throughout.

@runtime_checkable enables isinstance(obj, Trainer) checks during pipeline assembly, so the CLI and StageRunner can validate components at startup rather than at first use.

docs.title: SampleReader
docs.text:
class SampleReader(Protocol):
    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[Sample]: ...
    def __len__(self) -> int: ...

Produces Sample objects. __len__ must return a fast estimate (for progress reporting); it does not have to force full iteration.

docs.title: FeatureExtractor
docs.text:
class FeatureExtractor(Protocol):
    output_shape: tuple[int, ...] | None
    dtype: str
    def extract(self, sample: Sample) -> np.ndarray: ...

Stateless and pure. output_shape is None when the shape is dynamic (variable-length sequences). dtype is a numpy dtype string, e.g. "float32".

docs.title: EventLogger
docs.text:
class EventLogger(Protocol):
    def log_metric(self, name: str, value: float, step: int | None = None) -> None: ...
    def log_params(self, params: dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...
    def log_artifact(self, path: Path, artifact_path: str | None = None) -> None: ...
    def log_event(self, kind: str, **payload: Any) -> None: ...
    def set_tags(self, tags: dict[str, str]) -> None: ...

Passed into every layer. The CompositeEventLogger fans out to JSONL, stdout, and MLflow simultaneously.

docs.title: Trainer
docs.text:
class Trainer(Protocol):
    def fit(self, model, train, extractor, *, val=None, logger) -> TrainResult: ...
    def save(self, result: TrainResult, out_dir: Path) -> None: ...
    def load(self, model_dir: Path) -> Any: ...

Owns the full training loop plus serialization and deserialization.

docs.title: Evaluator
docs.text:
class Evaluator(Protocol):
    def evaluate(self, model, reader, extractor, *, logger) -> MetricReport: ...

Returns a MetricReport; does not write files (that is the runner's job).

docs.title: Predictor
docs.text:
class Predictor(Protocol):
    def predict(self, model, reader, extractor, *, out_path: Path, logger) -> Path: ...

Writes predictions.csv to out_path and returns the path.

See API Reference for full signatures.

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install maldet[lightning,mlflow]

docs.title: Scaffold a detector
docs.text:
maldet scaffold --template rf --name mydet --out ./mydet
cd ./mydet
pip install -e .
maldet check
maldet describe

docs.title: Write your features
docs.text:
Open src/mydet/features.py. Replace the ELF parse with your feature logic. The class must satisfy the FeatureExtractor protocol:

class MyFeatures:
    output_shape = (128,)
    dtype = "float32"
    def extract(self, sample): ...

docs.title: Train locally
docs.text:
Write a config.yaml:

defaults: [_self_]
stage: train
paths:
  config_dir: .
  output_dir: ./output
  samples_root: ./samples
  source_model: ./output/model
data:
  train_csv: ./train.csv
  test_csv: ./test.csv
  predict_csv: ./predict.csv
model:
  _target_: mydet.models.make_rf
  n_estimators: 100

Run:

maldet run train --config config.yaml

Artifacts land under ./output/ (model/, events.jsonl, metrics.json, manifest.json).

docs.title: maldet.types
docs.text: Core dataclasses shared across maldet layers.

docs.title: MetricReport dataclass
docs.text: Return value of Evaluator.evaluate().

docs.title: to_json_dict()
docs.text: Serialize into the wire shape of metrics.json (schema_version=1).

docs.title: Sample dataclass
docs.text:
A single binary sample.

label is None during predict. metadata is a per-instance mutable dict — frozen=True forbids reassigning the field, not mutating the contained dict.

docs.title: TrainResult dataclass
docs.text:
Return value of Trainer.fit().

model is the trained estimator or LightningModule. best_checkpoint is set by Lightning when a ModelCheckpoint callback ran. extras is a free-form dict that round-trips into events.

docs.title: maldet.protocols
docs.text:
Runtime-checkable Protocols for maldet's six layers + EventLogger.

Protocols use structural typing — implementations do not need to inherit. @runtime_checkable enables isinstance(obj, Trainer) for pipeline-assembly validation.

docs.title: maldet.events
docs.text:
CompositeEventLogger — fans out to N loggers, isolating failures.

JSONL event logger — append-only, fsync per event.

Stdout event logger — prefixed JSON line per event.

MLflow-backed event logger.

Keeps the mlflow import optional — if the caller passes mlflow=None and mlflow is not importable, log_* methods silently no-op. This makes MLflow a soft dependency (install maldet[mlflow] to enable).

docs.title: CompositeEventLogger
docs.text:
Forwards every call to every wrapped logger.

A delegate raising is caught and logged at WARNING; other delegates still run. This protects the training loop from a broken MLflow / filesystem from killing the detector run.

docs.title: JsonlEventLogger
docs.text:
Writes one NDJSON line per event to path.

Each write is followed by os.fsync so a pod kill does not lose events in the page cache. Parent directory is created if missing.

docs.title: StdoutEventLogger
docs.text: Writes maldet.event: {json}\n lines to stdout.

docs.title: log_event(kind, **payload)
docs.text:
Flatten event payload into tags named maldet.<kind>.<field>.

Metric events are NOT forwarded here — the caller calls log_metric for those.

docs.title: maldet.builtins
docs.text:
Built-in sample readers.

Built-in predictor: batch prediction over a SampleReader.

docs.title: SampleCsvReader
docs.text:
Reads a sample_csv contract CSV: columns file_name[,label].

Resolves each sample path under samples_root/<sha[:2]>/<sha>.

When strict=False (default), missing sample files are skipped with no error (lolday frequently produces CSVs that reference samples not yet present; platform guarantees the SHA is valid, not that the byte stream is).

docs.title: BatchPredictor
docs.text:
Iterate samples, extract features, call model.predict in one batch.

Writes a CSV with the required columns file_name, pred_label, pred_score. Extra columns are added as pred_prob_<class> when predict_proba is available.

docs.title: maldet.evaluators
docs.text: Binary-classification evaluator.

docs.title: BinaryClassification
docs.text:
Binary classification metrics using sklearn.metrics.

Runs model.predict once over the whole reader. Optionally calls predict_proba if available to compute ROC-AUC.

Labels are encoded via classes.index(sample.label) so that the evaluator's internal y matches the trainer's encoding for the same sample.label regardless of class ordering. pos_label is then threaded through sklearn metrics as the positive class index, so flipping classes order does not flip metric values.

docs.title: maldet.trainers
docs.text:
SklearnTrainer — thin wrapper around sklearn estimator.fit/predict/proba.

Lightning-based Trainer for deep-learning detectors.

Reads the platform-injected env vars MALDET_GPU_COUNT and MALDET_DISTRIBUTED_STRATEGY to pick accelerator, devices, and strategy for lightning.Trainer.

docs.title: SklearnTrainer
docs.text: Trainer for scikit-learn-compatible estimators (fit + predict).

docs.title: LightningTrainer
docs.text: PyTorch Lightning-based Trainer.

docs.title: MaldetLightningLogger
docs.text:
Bases: Logger

Adapter from Lightning's Logger API onto maldet EventLogger.

docs.title: MaldetProgressCallback
docs.text:
Bases: Callback

Emits epoch_begin / epoch_end events through the EventLogger.

docs.title: maldet.manifest
docs.text: Detector manifest — Pydantic model for maldet.toml and helpers.

docs.title: DetectorManifest
docs.text:
Bases: _Frozen

The full manifest (maldet.toml root).

docs.title: ManifestNotFoundError
docs.text:
Bases: FileNotFoundError

Raised by search_manifest when no maldet.toml is discoverable.

docs.title: search_manifest()
docs.text: Return the first manifest path found in: 1. $MALDET_MANIFEST env var (absolute path) 2. $PWD/maldet.toml 3. /app/maldet.toml (the scaffold Docker WORKDIR)

docs.title: maldet.runner
docs.text: StageRunner — loads manifest, composes layers via manifest symbols, drives stage.

docs.title: StageRunner
docs.text: Orchestrates a single stage (train / evaluate / predict).

docs.title: Scaffolding
docs.text: maldet scaffold generates a complete detector repository from a Jinja2 template. The output is a pip-installable Python package that satisfies the maldet protocol contracts out of the box.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
maldet scaffold --template <rf|cnn> --name <detector_name> --out <directory>
Option Default Description --template rf Template to use: rf or cnn --name (required) Package and detector name --out . Parent directory for the generated tree

docs.title: Templates
docs.text:
rf — Random Forest baseline using SklearnTrainer. Generates: a pyproject.toml, maldet.toml, src/<name>/features.py, src/<name>/models.py, config.yaml, and Dockerfile.

cnn — PyTorch Lightning CNN using LightningTrainer. Generates the same structure with a LightningModule stub and Lightning-specific config.

docs.title: After scaffolding
docs.text:
cd <out>
pip install -e .
maldet check      # validates maldet.toml
maldet describe   # prints manifest summary

Edit src/<name>/features.py to implement your feature extraction logic.

docs.title: Stages
docs.text: maldet supports three stages. Each stage is driven by maldet run <stage> --config <path>.

docs.title: train
docs.text:
Loads data.train_csv, materializes features, calls Trainer.fit(), and writes artifacts to paths.output_dir:

  • model/ — serialized estimator or Lightning checkpoint directory
  • events.jsonl — full event stream
  • manifest.json — snapshot of maldet.toml for provenance

docs.title: evaluate
docs.text: Loads paths.source_model, runs the model over data.test_csv, calls Evaluator.evaluate(), and writes metrics.json.

docs.title: predict
docs.text: Loads paths.source_model, runs the model over data.predict_csv, calls Predictor.predict(), and writes predictions.csv.

docs.title: maldet.toml fields
docs.text: maldet.toml is the detector manifest. It must be present in the working directory or at $MALDET_MANIFEST.

docs.title: [detector]
docs.text: Field Type Required name string yes version string yes framework sklearn | lightning | sklearn+lightning yes description string no

docs.title: [input]
docs.text: Field Type Default binary_format elf | pe | apk | raw_bytes required required_sections list[str] [] dataset_contract string "sample_csv"

docs.title: [output]
docs.text: Field Type Default task binary_classification | multiclass_classification | ... required classes list[str] [] score_range [float, float] [0.0, 1.0]

docs.title: [resources]
docs.text: Field Type Default supports list of cpu/gpu1/gpu2/gpu4/gpu8 ["cpu"] recommended string "cpu" min_memory_gib int 1 gpu_required bool false

docs.title: [lifecycle]
docs.text: Field Type Default stages list ["train", "evaluate", "predict"] supports_serving bool false supports_hpsweep bool true supports_distributed bool | "ddp" | "fsdp" false supports_multinode bool false

docs.title: [artifacts]
docs.text: Defines the expected output paths for model, metrics, and predictions.

docs.title: [compat]
docs.text: Field Default min_python "3.12" min_maldet "1.0" schema_version 1

docs.title: [stages.<name>]
docs.text:
Each stage block declares dotted-import paths (module:Class) for the layer symbols to instantiate:

[stages.train]
reader    = "mydet.readers:MyReader"
extractor = "mydet.features:MyFeatures"
model     = "mydet.models:make_rf"
trainer   = "maldet.trainers.sklearn_trainer:SklearnTrainer"

[stages.evaluate]
evaluator = "maldet.evaluators.binary:BinaryClassificationEvaluator"

[stages.predict]
predictor = "maldet.builtins.predictors:BatchPredictor"

docs.title: Integration with lolday
docs.text:
The lolday backend imports DetectorManifest from maldet.manifest to validate the base64-decoded io.maldet.manifest OCI image label. When lolday Phase 11b ships, it pins maldet ~= 1.0 in its backend/pyproject.toml, gaining type-safe manifest validation for free.

Platform-side, lolday also reads:

  • /mnt/output/events.jsonl — via a sidecar tail that POSTs each event to the internal /internal/jobs/{job_id}/events endpoint
  • /mnt/output/manifest.json — stored alongside MLflow artifacts for provenance
  • /mnt/output/metrics.json — parsed as the canonical evaluation record
  • /mnt/output/predictions.csv — served back to the user via /api/v1/runs/{run_id}/artifacts/download

For the Volcano Job spec that mounts these paths and invokes maldet run <stage>, see the lolday repo's services/job_spec.py (Phase 11b onward).
